source_thresholds.label: PPGL biochemical confirmation: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines above assay ULN need follow-up; values about 3-fold ULN are high probability
PPGL biochemical confirmation: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines above assay ULN need follow-up; values about 3-fold ULN are high probability
PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease

Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
Plasma free metanephrines or urinary fractionated metanephrines.
Use proper sampling conditions; supine rest for plasma when possible.
Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.

PPGL safety tests: obtain plasma free or urine fractionated metanephrines under appropriate collection conditions, add ECG/cardiac injury testing for crisis symptoms, and reserve imaging/genetic pathway for biochemical framing unless emergency stabilization is needed.
Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL.

Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context.
Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive.
>3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway.

Pheochromocytoma: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup

Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.

Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics.
>3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.
Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable.

criteria_options.label: PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends preoperative blockade for hormonally functional PPGL and medical treatment for 7 to 14 days to normalize blood pressure/heart rate, with high-sodium diet and fluid intake to prevent postoperative hypotension.

criteria_options.label: PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most adrenal pheochromocytomas and open resection for large, for example >6 cm, or invasive pheochromocytomas.

criteria_options.label: PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society remarks that biochemical testing to document successful removal should occur after recovery, for example 2-4 weeks after surgery, and recommends lifelong annual biochemical testing for recurrent or metastatic PPGL.

criteria_options.label: PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends preoperative blockade for hormonally functional PPGL and medical treatment for 7 to 14 days to normalize blood pressure/heart rate, with high-sodium diet and fluid intake to prevent postoperative hypotension.

criteria_options.label: PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most adrenal pheochromocytomas and open resection for large, for example >6 cm, or invasive pheochromocytomas.

criteria_options.label: PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society remarks that biochemical testing to document successful removal should occur after recovery, for example 2-4 weeks after surgery, and recommends lifelong annual biochemical testing for recurrent or metastatic PPGL.

children.action: Hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, ACS/stroke symptoms, planned surgery without alpha blockade.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE.; Measure blood pressure. Begin alpha-adrenergic blockade and volume/salt preparation before surgery once diagnosis is established; add beta-blocker only after adequate alpha blockade if tachyarrhythmia requires it.; Refer for adrenalectomy or tumor resection with endocrine, anesthesia, and surgical planning.; Use genetic counseling/testing when PPGL is confirmed or hereditary features are present.; Manage hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, ACS/stroke symptoms, or planned urgent procedure with specialist/ICU-level support.; >3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

guideline_cutoffs.label: PPGL biochemical confirmation: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines above assay ULN need follow-up; values about 3-fold ULN are high probability
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines for initial PPGL testing, with follow-up based on extent of increase and clinical presentation; large elevations such as 3-fold ULN are high probability while borderline results require preanalytical review.
data_needed: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Biochemical testing recommendations 1.1-1.4

guideline_cutoffs.label: PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends preoperative blockade for hormonally functional PPGL and medical treatment for 7 to 14 days to normalize blood pressure/heart rate, with high-sodium diet and fluid intake to prevent postoperative hypotension.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Perioperative medical management recommendations 4.1-4.2

guideline_cutoffs.label: PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most adrenal pheochromocytomas and open resection for large, for example >6 cm, or invasive pheochromocytomas.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Surgery recommendation 5.1

guideline_cutoffs.label: PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society remarks that biochemical testing to document successful removal should occur after recovery, for example 2-4 weeks after surgery, and recommends lifelong annual biochemical testing for recurrent or metastatic PPGL.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Follow-up recommendation 4.4 remarks

criteria_options.label: PPGL biochemical confirmation: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines above assay ULN need follow-up; values about 3-fold ULN are high probability
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines for initial PPGL testing, with follow-up based on extent of increase and clinical presentation; large elevations such as 3-fold ULN are high probability while borderline results require preanalytical review.
data_needed: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines

criteria_options.label: PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends preoperative blockade for hormonally functional PPGL and medical treatment for 7 to 14 days to normalize blood pressure/heart rate, with high-sodium diet and fluid intake to prevent postoperative hypotension.

criteria_options.label: PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most adrenal pheochromocytomas and open resection for large, for example >6 cm, or invasive pheochromocytomas.

criteria_options.label: PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society remarks that biochemical testing to document successful removal should occur after recovery, for example 2-4 weeks after surgery, and recommends lifelong annual biochemical testing for recurrent or metastatic PPGL.

children.label: Pheochromocytoma: de-escalate, stop, or narrow when objective criteria normalize
children.edgeLabel: Pheochromocytoma objective response supports narrowing, stopping, tapering, or continuing: >ULN; 3-fold ULN; 7 to 14 days; >6 cm; >3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway.; Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Begin alpha-adrenergic blockade and volume/salt preparation before surgery once diagnosis is established; add beta-blocker only after adequate alpha blockade if tachyarrhythmia requires it.

criteria.description: Stop, narrow, taper, or de-escalate Pheochromocytoma treatment only when objective response, diagnosis certainty, and cited stopping criteria support it.

criteria_options.label: PPGL biochemical confirmation: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines above assay ULN need follow-up; values about 3-fold ULN are high probability
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines for initial PPGL testing, with follow-up based on extent of increase and clinical presentation; large elevations such as 3-fold ULN are high probability while borderline results require preanalytical review.
data_needed: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines

criteria_options.label: PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends preoperative blockade for hormonally functional PPGL and medical treatment for 7 to 14 days to normalize blood pressure/heart rate, with high-sodium diet and fluid intake to prevent postoperative hypotension.

criteria_options.label: PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most adrenal pheochromocytomas and open resection for large, for example >6 cm, or invasive pheochromocytomas.

criteria_options.label: PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease
criteria_options.criteria_text: Endocrine Society remarks that biochemical testing to document successful removal should occur after recovery, for example 2-4 weeks after surgery, and recommends lifelong annual biochemical testing for recurrent or metastatic PPGL.

parallel_actions: Begin alpha-adrenergic blockade and volume/salt preparation before surgery once diagnosis is established; add beta-blocker only after adequate alpha blockade if tachyarrhythmia requires it.
Refer for adrenalectomy or tumor resection with endocrine, anesthesia, and surgical planning.
Use genetic counseling/testing when PPGL is confirmed or hereditary features are present.
Manage hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, ACS/stroke symptoms, or planned urgent procedure with specialist/ICU-level support.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Pheochromocytoma; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
>3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway.
Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.
Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

guideline_cutoffs.label: PPGL biochemical confirmation: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines above assay ULN need follow-up; values about 3-fold ULN are high probability
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines for initial PPGL testing, with follow-up based on extent of increase and clinical presentation; large elevations such as 3-fold ULN are high probability while borderline results require preanalytical review.
data_needed: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Biochemical testing recommendations 1.1-1.4

guideline_cutoffs.label: PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends preoperative blockade for hormonally functional PPGL and medical treatment for 7 to 14 days to normalize blood pressure/heart rate, with high-sodium diet and fluid intake to prevent postoperative hypotension.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Perioperative medical management recommendations 4.1-4.2

guideline_cutoffs.label: PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most adrenal pheochromocytomas and open resection for large, for example >6 cm, or invasive pheochromocytomas.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Surgery recommendation 5.1

guideline_cutoffs.label: PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Endocrine Society remarks that biochemical testing to document successful removal should occur after recovery, for example 2-4 weeks after surgery, and recommends lifelong annual biochemical testing for recurrent or metastatic PPGL.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Follow-up recommendation 4.4 remarks

clinical_criteria.label: PPGL biochemical confirmation: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines above assay ULN need follow-up; values about 3-fold ULN are high probability
clinical_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines for initial PPGL testing, with follow-up based on extent of increase and clinical presentation; large elevations such as 3-fold ULN are high probability while borderline results require preanalytical review.
data_needed: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines

clinical_criteria.label: PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
clinical_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends preoperative blockade for hormonally functional PPGL and medical treatment for 7 to 14 days to normalize blood pressure/heart rate, with high-sodium diet and fluid intake to prevent postoperative hypotension.

clinical_criteria.label: PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
clinical_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most adrenal pheochromocytomas and open resection for large, for example >6 cm, or invasive pheochromocytomas.

clinical_criteria.label: PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease
clinical_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society remarks that biochemical testing to document successful removal should occur after recovery, for example 2-4 weeks after surgery, and recommends lifelong annual biochemical testing for recurrent or metastatic PPGL.

Pheochromocytoma danger feature or high-risk cutoff: Hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, ACS/stroke symptoms, planned surgery without alpha blockade.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE.; Measure blood pressure; >ULN; 3-fold ULN; 7 to 14 days; >3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway.

Pheochromocytoma mimic/exclusion better fits: Pheochromocytoma: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup; Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.; Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics.

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: PPGL biochemical confirmation: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines above assay ULN need follow-up; values about 3-fold ULN are high probability
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines for initial PPGL testing, with follow-up based on extent of increase and clinical presentation; large elevations such as 3-fold ULN are high probability while borderline results require preanalytical review.
data_needed: plasma free or urinary fractionated metanephrines

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: PPGL pre-op preparation: functional tumor needs alpha blockade and high-sodium/fluid preparation for 7 to 14 days before surgery
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends preoperative blockade for hormonally functional PPGL and medical treatment for 7 to 14 days to normalize blood pressure/heart rate, with high-sodium diet and fluid intake to prevent postoperative hypotension.

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: PPGL surgery approach: open resection for large pheochromocytoma >6 cm or invasive disease; minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most smaller adrenal tumors
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society recommends minimally invasive adrenalectomy for most adrenal pheochromocytomas and open resection for large, for example >6 cm, or invasive pheochromocytomas.

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: PPGL follow-up: biochemical testing 2-4 weeks after surgery and lifelong annual metanephrine testing for recurrence/metastatic disease
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: Endocrine Society remarks that biochemical testing to document successful removal should occur after recovery, for example 2-4 weeks after surgery, and recommends lifelong annual biochemical testing for recurrent or metastatic PPGL.

clinical_cutoff_review.review_note: Cutoff rows were added because the prior module evidence did not expose enough explicit guideline thresholds for pathway traversal.
